behavior_summary: U8P_KEGEW::look is the object's U8P_KEGEW::look/examine handler. When the player inspects this object/NPC, the script emits a single short bark text: "keg ".
Immediately after speaking the bark the process yields/ends (the script issues a suspend/terminate).
There are no menus, conditionals, flag reads/writes, parameters, or external calls in this excerpt — it is a minimal descriptive handler.
notable_control_flow: Linear: entry -> Bark("keg ") -> suspend/terminate. No branching or loops.
The presence of two raw/Unknown nodes surrounding the bark suggests minimal wrapper/usecode artifacts, but they do not change observable behavior.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "keg " intentional (part of a longer concatenated label) or an artifact of extraction? (confidence: medium)
Is this object an item, scene prop, or NPC (the script is a U8P_KEGEW::look handler and does not disambiguate type)? (confidence: low)
Do the Unknown nodes correspond to engine metadata (enter/leave fast-area) or leftover decompiler markers that could alter invocation context? (confidence: low)
